mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 4% signals weakening labor market conditions, reducing corporate profit expectations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4%, or the correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns falls below -0.2 in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) indicates strong demand pressures and inflation, prompting central bank tightening that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show positive correlation with both GDPC1 and CPIAUCSL in more than 50% of observations, or the interaction term between GDPC1 and CPIAUCSL fails to explain >15% of SPX return variance.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals supply-side shocks, creating stagflationary pressures that drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show positive correlation with both INDPRO and CPIAUCSL in more than 50% of observations, or the interaction term between INDPRO and CPIAUCSL fails to explain >10% of SPX return variance.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly job gains (PAYEMS) above 150k signal robust labor market conditions, boosting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in >40% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 150k, or the correlation between PAYEMS and SPX returns falls below 0.15 in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1200k signals weakening demand for construction, reducing corporate investment and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in >50% of instances where HOUST falls below 1200, or the correlation between HOUST and SPX returns falls below -0.1 in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) and falling real savings (RSAFS) indicates deteriorating purchasing power, prompting monetary tightening that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show positive correlation with both CPIAUCSL and RSAFS in more than 50% of observations, or the interaction term between CPIAUCSL and RSAFS fails to explain >12% of SPX return variance.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong demand conditions, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during expansionary regimes.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative correlation with both RSAFS and HOUST in more than 50% of observations, or the interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST fails to explain >18% of SPX return variance.
